description_long: This is a roughly hewn, upright stone with an irregular, block-like shape and a rugged, natural surface. The stone displays a mix of gray and reddish-brown hues, with visible textural variations and some weathered edges. One face of the stone features a hand-written marking or number in black ink, suggesting it may have been cataloged or used for identification. The sides are uneven, with one edge appearing notably thinner and more angular than the others. The surface is matte and coarse, with no signs of artificial polishing or smoothing. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a boundary marker, obstacle, or decorative element in an urban or landscaped setting.
description: A rugged, upright stone with gray and reddish-brown tones, featuring a hand-written marking and a coarse, natural surface.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broadest face of the stone, with a visible hand-written marking and a mix of gray and reddish-brown coloration.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the stone's narrowest profile, highlighting its angular, uneven edge and the rough, textured surface.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a mostly flat, grayish surface with subtle reddish patches and no visible markings.
description_view_3: This opposite side view again shows the stone's thin edge, with pronounced surface irregularities and a blend of colors.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or boundary marker, upright on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and color variation, with a hand-written marking on one face; no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with some edges rounded and surface showing signs of natural exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or landscaped urban environments, but may blend with other natural stones or boundary features.
